title: [Bank] IG02 — high CPU usage

Its maxmemory is capped at 4GB (4294967296 bytes) while used_memory_peak is only ~50MB, suggesting a misconfiguration or that Redis01 is being hammered with commands far beyond normal operating baseline. By contrast, Redis02 (green) shows a higher used_memory_peak (~95MB) and used_memory (~13MB) but far fewer anomalies, indicating Redis01 is the unhealthy node in the cache tier. (2) The load pattern is consistent with a traffic surge or load-test: apache_access_log and localhost_access_log entries show k6/0.29.0 (a load-testing tool) actively generating POST traffic to the UOCP application endpoints starting at 01:38:00. This is the most significant change event visible in the data \u2014 a k6-driven load test was initiated at ~01:38:00, injecting sustained high-volume traffic into the stack. (3) The Tomcat tier (Tomcat01\u201304) all show elevated JVM max memory at 4187619328 bytes (~3.9GB), high ProcessingTimeRequestInfo values (up to ~957M ms on Tomcat01), and high MaxTimeRequestInfo (~324\u2013330 seconds), indicating severe request queuing and processing delays. These are consistent with a downstream cache miss amplification or Redis01 becoming a bottleneck \u2014 when Redis01 degrades, Tomcat instances fall back to slower data paths (MySQL), increasing processing time. (4) Tomcat02 GC logs show repeated Allocation Failures starting at 01:38:44, with ParNew collections running every ~60\u201368 seconds, indicating the JVM young generation is being saturated continuously \u2014 a symptom of high request throughput overwhelming the application tier, not an application bug in isolation. (5) MG01 and MG02 (middleware gateway services) show high NoHeapMemoryUsed (~507MB and ~947MB respectively) and high network throughput (~635KB/s and ~536KB/s), consistent with being intermediary services absorbing the traffic spike and propagating load downstream. (6) IG01 and IG02 (ingress gateway services) show high NETPacketsIn/Out (~403M packets each) and are the entry points receiving k6 traffic, confirming the traffic originates externally and flows through IG \u2192 MG \u2192 Tomcat \u2192 Redis/MySQL. (8) apache01 filesystem metrics show root partition available space at only 7224 MB with used at 7066 MB at 02:22:00, indicating potential disk pressure on the web tier, but this is likely a secondary effect of log accumulation under load rather than the initiating cause. Propagation chain: k6 load test initiated at 01:38:00 \u2192 IG01/IG02 absorb traffic spike \u2192 MG01/MG02 route requests \u2192 Tomcat01\u201304 experience JVM memory pressure and GC stress \u2192 Redis01 becomes saturated (commands, connections, RDB change backlog) \u2192 cache layer degrades \u2192 Tomcat falls back to MySQL \u2192 MySQL slow queries emerge \u2192 end-to-end latency spikes \u2192 apache01 alert fires."}

title: [Bank] Redis02 — high CPU usage
